articles.title: Should you work on your own games as an external development studio?
articles.summary: External development studios can co-exist with internal projects, but working on external games as a primary focus may lead to demotivation and lack of personal satisfaction. Redcatpig's Bryan Freitas believes that the best work comes from people building something they believe in, while João Toste thinks internally made games provide valuable experience for growth. Working on internal projects can also give studios creative freedom to experiment with new styles and techniques, as well as firsthand experience of game development complexities.
